description: BASELINE: 2 passes out of 197 valid test files (glslangValidator reference). Script now uses glslangValidator to classify files as VALID/INVALID/SKIP, and only tests our compiler against VALID files. Fixed false-positive PASS detection (summary line "PASS: 0" was matching grep). Real breakdown: 2 pass, 101 compile_error, 15 spirv-val fail, 79 hangs, 0 crashes.

asi.hypothesis: Establish true baseline with glslangValidator reference filtering
key_findings: struct-varying.legacy.frag appears as PASS but has double-free memory corruption - GPA prints error but exits 0
PASS detection was broken: grep matched 'PASS: 0' in summary line, giving ~214 false positives before fix
Ghostty .glsl files need -S frag/vert flag for glslangValidator classification
common.glsl is include-only, not standalone - added to skip list
Hangs are biggest category (79/197 = 40%), parser infinite loops on unexpected constructs
asi.next_action_hint: Fix hangs first (biggest bang for buck), then compile errors, then spirv-val failures. Phase 1: stability.

description: Fix parser hang: synchronize() now consumes r_brace. 79 hangs → 0, 2→7 passes. Root cause: r_brace was a sync point but wasn't consumed, so parseTopLevel() kept failing on the same token forever.

asi.breakdown: 7 pass, 159 compile_error, 26 spirv_val_fail, 0 hang, 5 crash out of 197
asi.delta: +5 pass, +58 compile_error (from hangs converted), -79 hangs, +11 spirv_val_fail, +5 crash
asi.fix: synchronize() in parser.zig now consumes r_brace instead of returning without advancing. Root cause: parseTopLevel() failed on r_brace, synchronize() saw r_brace and returned without consuming it -> infinite loop
asi.hypothesis: Fix parser synchronize() r_brace handling to prevent infinite loops
asi.next_action_hint: Fix 5 crashes (memory corruption), then tackle compile errors

description: Fix struct member double-free: duplicate members in TypeDef. 5 crashes → 0, 7→9 passes.

asi.fix: semantic.zig: duplicate struct members slice in TypeDef instead of sharing with AST. Fixes double-free when both freeTree() and module.deinit() free the same slice.
asi.hypothesis: Fix double-free by duplicating struct members in semantic collectTopLevel
asi.next_action_hint: Tackle compile errors - biggest category at 159/197. Look at common patterns causing compile errors.

description: Fix OpEntryPoint interface variables + skip preprocessor directives. 9→13 passes, spirv-val fails 29→25.

asi.fix1: parser.zig: skip pp_version/pp_define/etc tokens at top level, consume version number and profile
asi.fix2: codegen.zig: add Input/Output global variable IDs to OpEntryPoint operands per SPIR-V spec
asi.hypothesis: Fix OpEntryPoint interface variables + skip preprocessor directives in parser
asi.next_action_hint: Continue reducing compile errors. 159/197 still failing. Check common patterns.

description: Add uniform_block handling, gl_Position builtin, precision keyword. 13 passes (unchanged), 3 files moved from compile_error to spirv-val fail.

asi.changes: Added uniform_block semantic handling, gl_Position builtin, precision keyword in lexer, skip precision in parser
asi.delta: same pass, -3 compile_error (moved to spirv_val_fail), +3 spirv_val_fail
asi.hypothesis: Add uniform_block support, gl_Position builtin, precision keyword handling
asi.next_action_hint: Uniform blocks produce SPIR-V but fail spirv-val - need Block decoration + member offsets. Focus on spirv-val failures next - many are OpEntryPoint fixed, check remaining ones.

description: RESUME: Re-establish baseline at 13 passes after context limit.

asi.next_action: Fix top-level var_decl Function storage class (causes 8 spirv-val failures), then fix sampler2D type encoding, then tackle compile errors

description: Fix top-level var_decl storage class (Private not Function) + fix sampler2D OpTypeSampledImage word count. 13→18 passes.

asi.fix1: ir.zig: add private storage class (6). semantic.zig: top-level var_decl uses Private instead of Function storage class
asi.fix2: codegen.zig: fix OpTypeSampledImage word count 4→3, fix multisampled flag 1→0 for regular 2D textures
asi.hypothesis: Fix top-level var_decl storage class + sampler type encoding

description: Fix scalar-to-vector splat, GLSL builtin return type inference, float detection for vectors. 18→21 passes.

asi.fix1: semantic.zig: scalar-to-vector splat in type_constructor (vec4(1.0) now emits 4 operands)
asi.fix2: semantic.zig: GLSL builtin return type inferred from first arg (round(vec4)→vec4)
asi.fix3: semantic.zig: is_float checks include vectors/matrices for binary_op, compound_assign, unary_op

description: Add buffer/SSBO keywords, more GL builtins, texture function handling. 21 passes (unchanged).

asi.changes: Added texture/buffer/readonly/writeonly/coherent/restrict keywords and handling. Added gl_Layer/gl_ViewportIndex/gl_VertexID/gl_InstanceID builtins.
asi.issue: Texture functions added but files using them still fail for other reasons. Need to trace actual failure point per file.
asi.next_action_hint: Add error tracing to identify which pipeline stage fails per file. Focus on top-20 most impactful compile errors.

description: Add stage detection from file extension in runner. 21 passes (unchanged but infrastructure improvement).

asi.changes: runner.zig now detects stage from file extension (.vert→vertex, .comp→compute, else→fragment)
asi.next_action_hint: Need deeper investigation. Modify compileToSPIRV to report which stage fails (lex/parse/semantic/codegen). Then fix the highest-frequency failure reason.

description: Declare uniform block members as accessible symbols + relax promoteTypes. 155→132 compile errors, 23 files now produce SPIR-V output.

asi.delta: same pass, -23 compile_error, +23 spirv_val_fail (moved from error to producing SPIR-V)
asi.fix1: semantic.zig: uniform_block members now declared as directly accessible symbols
asi.fix2: semantic.zig: promoteTypes relaxed - handles mat*vec, vec*scalar, falls back to left type
asi.key_insight: All compile errors are in semantic stage. Relaxed type promotion + uniform block member access unblocks 23 files.
asi.next_action_hint: The 44 spirv-val failures are close to passes. Many will be OpEntryPoint (now fixed) or undefined IDs from uniform block member access chains not being properly emitted.

description: Register GL builtins as proper SPIR-V globals. 21→22 passes.

asi.fix: Register GL builtins (gl_Position, gl_FragCoord, gl_FragColor, gl_Layer, gl_ViewportIndex, gl_VertexID, gl_InstanceID) as proper globals with Input/Output storage class, not just symbols. This ensures OpVariable is emitted for them and IDs are defined.
asi.next_action_hint: 22 passes. 132 compile errors remain. The OpIMul %void bug for mat*vec still exists - basic.vert still shows this despite having correct types in semantic analysis. Need to trace why the binary_op gets wrong tag.

description: Stable at 22 passes after cleanup. Found parser AST issue: binary_op nesting for uMVP expression.

asi.findings: Debugging basic.vert showed the parser produces a nested binary_op where uMVP's expression analysis returns void. The left child of the outer binary_op is itself a binary_op node, which doesn't match expected AST for 'uMVP * aVertex'. More investigation needed into parser AST structure for assignment expressions. The block_member access chain + storage class fix infrastructure is in place.
asi.next_action_hint: Dump the AST for basic.vert to understand why uMVP ends up inside nested binary_op. The parser might produce unexpected AST for 'gl_Position = uMVP * aVertex'. Focus on a different area while this is investigated.

description: Handle in/out interface blocks with proper storage class. 22 passes (unchanged).

asi.changes: Parser handles in/out blocks, semantic uses Input/Output storage class based on qualifier
asi.next_action_hint: Need to investigate why compile errors are so persistent. Maybe add a diagnostic mode to the runner that prints which semantic error occurs. Consider implementing the #include preprocessor to unblock Ghostty shaders.

description: Fix critical parser evaluation-order bug. 22→24 passes.

asi.findings: Zig 0.15.2 evaluation order issue: 'left = .{ .data = .{ .children = dupeNodes(&.{left, right}) } }' causes left to be read AFTER the assignment corrupts it. makeBinaryOp() helper fixes this by capturing operands in separate const bindings before constructing the node. This affected ALL binary operators in the parser, meaning ALL expressions with 2+ operands were potentially corrupted. This explains the void-type cascade in UBO member access.
asi.hypothesis: Fix evaluation-order bug in parser binary op creation that corrupts AST nodes
asi.next_action_hint: The makeBinaryOp fix affects ALL binary expressions. Many spirv-val failures (41) and compile errors (132) may now have different root causes since the AST is correct. Re-examine remaining failures.

description: Add mat*vec SPIR-V ops + Uniform in OpEntryPoint. 24→25 passes.

asi.findings: Uniform storage class globals must also be listed in OpEntryPoint interface list. Also added MatrixTimesVector/VectorTimesMatrix/MatrixTimesMatrix for proper mat*vec multiplication.
asi.hypothesis: Add mat*vec ops and Uniform storage class to OpEntryPoint interface list
asi.next_action_hint: Check remaining 10 FMul errors (some might now be fixed by mat_vec ops). Then tackle 'block must end with branch' (5 files) and 'Result Type int or float vector' (4 files).

description: Handle vec*scalar and scalar*vec multiplication. 25→27 passes.

asi.findings: scalar*vec and vec*scalar must use OpVectorTimesScalar (not FMul). For scalar*vec, operands must be swapped so vector is first. Also handles compound_assign mul_assign case.
asi.hypothesis: Handle vec*scalar and scalar*vec multiplication correctly
asi.next_action_hint: Continue reducing spirv-val failures: 5 'block must end with branch', 4 'Result Type int or float vector'. Also start looking at compile errors more systematically.

description: Add #extension support + fix block comments. 29→38 passes.

asi.findings: 51 of 132 compile errors were lex failures! Two root causes: (1) #extension directive not recognized by lexer → InvalidToken error. (2) Block comment parser used nesting (GLSL doesn't nest block comments). Fixing both recovered 51 lex failures, though 34 moved to other error categories (parse/semantic). Adding pp_extension token + directive map entry fixed the #extension issue. Removing nesting from parseBlockComment fixed the comment issue. Also added diagnostic output to categorize errors by stage (lex_failed vs semantic_failed).
asi.hypothesis: Fix lexer failures: #extension and block comment nesting
asi.next_action_hint: Re-categorize remaining 115 compile errors. Many lex failures may now be parse or semantic errors. Also check the 8 new spirv-val failures.

description: Add compute builtins + local_size + BuiltIn decorations. 38 passes (unchanged).

asi.findings: Added compute shader builtins and LocalSize support, but files using them still fail at semantic for other reasons (missing atomicAdd, buffer block member access, etc.). The new BuiltIn decoration emission was needed but gl_Layer/gl_ViewportIndex broke things by requiring Geometry capability. Had to disable those. The diagnostics show all 115 remaining compile errors are semantic_failed.
asi.hypothesis: Add compute builtins and local_size_x layout parsing
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on reducing the 115 semantic errors. Need to understand what specific semantic features are missing. Try adding a function that returns specific error messages from semantic analysis.

description: Add vector-to-vector conversion. 38 pass, fixes 2 spirv-val Constituents errors.

asi.findings: Constituents error fixed - vec4(ivec4) uses ConvertSToF now
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on interface variable listing errors

description: Fix uniform block parsing + access chain constant indices. 38→45 passes (+7).

asi.findings: Two critical fixes: (1) uniform block parsing was broken because tryType() consumed the block name before the { could be detected. Fixed by checking for blocks before tryType(). (2) OpAccessChain requires index operands to be OpConstant IDs, not raw literals. Fixed by using emitIntConstant(). The old synchronize() workaround for uniform blocks accidentally worked but produced wrong storage class. Proper block parsing + access chains is the correct path.
asi.hypothesis: Fix uniform block parsing and access chain codegen
asi.next_action_hint: Categorize remaining 36 spirv-val failures and 116 compile errors. The 48 empty-ctx semantic failures likely need specific unhandled AST node types.

description: List ALL globals in OpEntryPoint (SPIR-V 1.4+). 45→53 passes (+8).

asi.findings: SPIR-V 1.4+ requires ALL global variables used by entry point to be listed in OpEntryPoint interface list. Previously only Input/Output/Uniform were listed. Private globals were missing.
asi.hypothesis: List all globals in OpEntryPoint for SPIR-V 1.4+ compliance
asi.next_action_hint: 28 spirv-val failures remain. Top categories: 'block must end with branch' (3), 'Operand requires previous definition' (5), type mismatches, various. Also 116 compile errors to address.

description: Add 'invariant' keyword as qualifier. 53→54 passes (+1).

asi.findings: Added invariant keyword handling. Simple lexer + parser change.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on 'Operand requires previous definition' (6 files) or compile errors. The 'in-block-qualifiers' error needs investigation.

description: Fix pointer type cache collision for named types + scalar identity. 54→55 passes.

asi.findings: Named struct types collided in ensurePointerType cache because @intFromEnum(.named) is same for all named types. Fixed by hashing name into key. Also fixed scalar-to-scalar identity in type_constructor.
asi.hypothesis: Fix named type pointer cache collision
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on 26 remaining spirv-val failures or 116 compile errors. The 'block must end with branch' (3) and 'Operand requires previous definition' (5) are good targets.

description: Add scalar type conversion support. 55→56 passes (+1).

asi.findings: Scalar type constructors (int(uint), float(int)) were silently returning the argument. Fixed by adding proper conversion instructions. Added ConvertFToU/ConvertSToU/ConvertUToS opcodes.
asi.hypothesis: Fix scalar type conversion for int(uint) etc.
asi.next_action_hint: 25 spirv-val failures, 116 compile errors. Try addressing 'block must end with branch' for remaining 2 files, or look at type mismatches in FMul/FAdd.

description: Add named type caching + OpTypeRuntimeArray. 56 passes (stable).

asi.findings: Array bracket parsing in uniform blocks causes regression because buffer blocks with unknown types (int32_t) get parsed instead of failing gracefully. Named type caching prevents duplicate OpTypeStruct emissions. Runtime array support ready for when bracket parsing is safe.
asi.hypothesis: Add named type caching + runtime arrays
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on spirv-val failures that don't need parser changes. 'Expected arithmetic operands' type mismatches, or the 47 generic SemanticFailed compile errors.

description: Fix promoteTypes ordering for vector/scalar. 56→57 passes (+1).

asi.findings: promoteTypes(float, vec4) returned float because scalar checks preceded vector checks. Moving vector/scalar promotion first fixes scalar*vector operations. This was causing OpVectorTimesScalar with wrong result type.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue fixing type mismatches. The 3 'block must end with branch' files need deeper investigation into parser/expression analysis interaction.

description: Session 6: promoteTypes fix + cleanup. 57 passes (stable).

asi.findings: Lexer fix for '.' tokenization causes 57→35 regression. The '.' being classified as double_literal is relied upon by the parser for member access (dot operator). Swizzle fix (load+composite_extract) also causes regression because it adds load instructions that break the instruction ordering in some cases. promoteTypes ordering fix is the main win this session.
asi.next_action_hint: The lexer '.' issue is deep and risky. Focus on compile errors (115). Add errdefer context to analyzeExpression to identify which AST nodes are failing. Then target the most common failing node types.

description: Fix ternary OpSelect + add roundEven builtin. 57→59 passes (+2).

asi.findings: ternary_op (?: operator) was creating phantom IDs. Now emits proper OpSelect instruction. roundEven was missing from isGLSLBuiltin list. Both fixes together account for +2 passes.
asi.hypothesis: Fix ternary OpSelect + add roundEven to builtins
asi.next_action_hint: Look for more phantom ID patterns — member_access on vectors, binary_op on void types, etc. These generate undefined IDs in SPIR-V.

description: OpTypeFunction ordering + param IDs. 59 passes (stable).

asi.findings: emitFunctions OpTypeFunction reordering: no pass count change but fixes SPIR-V structure. sampler.frag still fails due to opaque type (sampler2D) function parameters needing special handling. param_ids stored in IR.Function but doesn't change pass count yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Look at the 114 compile errors. Focus on patterns that affect many files. The errdefer shows: 46 assign_op, 34 func_call, 8 compound_assign, 7 type_constructor, 6 index_access.

description: Add GLSL builtins: inverse, outerProduct, floatBitsTo*, imageSize, modf, fma. 59→60 passes (+1).

asi.findings: Adding 25+ GLSL builtins resolved 7 compile errors but 6 now fail spirv-val with wrong result types
asi.next_action_hint: Fix bitcast builtin result types, image builtins

description: Revert uniform block [] parsing. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: Uniform block [] parsing causes regression because runtime array members get base type (vec4) instead of array type. When used as index_access target, produces invalid SPIR-V. Need proper array type handling in semantic analysis before re-enabling.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on fixing spirv-val failures (30) and the remaining 107 compile errors. The 'identifier' inner errors are the biggest category — check what identifiers are missing.

description: Barrier builtins as void stubs. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: Barrier builtins added as void stubs - one file now compiles but fails spirv-val. Need proper OpControlBarrier/OpMemoryBarrier implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Look at the 10 'identifier|assign_op' errors — these are undeclared identifiers in RHS of assignments. Find which identifiers are missing and add them.

description: sampler2DShadow + barrier builtins. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: sampler2DShadow added. Texture builtins (textureGather, texelFetchOffset, etc.) cause spirv-val regressions because they use image_fetch fallback instead of proper SPIR-V ops. Need proper OpImageGather/OpImageFetch with correct operands. Barrier builtins added as void stubs.
asi.next_action_hint: Look at fixing the 9 'assign_op|assign_op' errors — these are RHS expression failures NOT caused by undeclared identifiers. Check what specific semantic failures occur in simple expressions like compound initialization or nested function calls.

description: Revert [] parsing attempt #3. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: Uniform block [] parsing causes consistent regression. The parser change affects non-array blocks too. The is_runtime_array flag approach in semantic.zig didn't help because the real issue is the parser consuming tokens that shouldn't be consumed. Need to understand WHY 22 shaders regress when [] parsing is added. Possibly the [ token is consumed by other parser paths (e.g., fixed-size arrays in struct declarations or variable declarations) and the parseUniformBlock change interferes.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on the 31 spirv-val failures instead of compile errors. Look for phantom ID patterns, type mismatches, and missing OpLoad instructions. These are closer to passing.

description: Implement post/pre increment with load+add+store. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: post_increment now emits load+add+store instead of phantom ID. No pass change because files using i++ either already work (for-loop body succeeds) or have other issues preventing compilation.
asi.next_action_hint: The 31 spirv-val failures are the next frontier. Focus on 'Operand requires previous definition' (6 files) — these are likely phantom IDs from other sources. Check remaining phantom ID patterns in analyzeExpression.

description: imageSize + image2D + ImageQuery capability. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: imageSize now uses OpImageQuerySize. image2D type added. ImageQuery capability added. image-size.frag error improved from 'undefined ID' to 'operand cannot be type'. Still 60 passes overall but infrastructure improvements for future image-related fixes.
asi.next_action_hint: Look at the remaining 'Operand requires previous definition' files. Also investigate why post_increment doesn't help pass count — check if for-loops use i++ as the increment.

description: Fix void main(void) parsing. spirv-val 31→29, compile 106→108. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: void main(void) parameter parsing fixed. spirv-val 31→29, compile 106→108. The void backtracking might affect functions like 'void foo(invariant vec4 v4)' where 'invariant' is consumed as qualifier before 'void' check. Actually no, void is checked before the while loop, so it only matches when void is the FIRST token after '('.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate the 2 new compile errors. Also check if the 29 spirv-val failures have any easy wins — especially the 'execution model' errors and 'Duplicate type declarations'.

description: Add mediump/highp/lowp precision qualifiers. 60 passes (stable).

asi.findings: mediump/highp/lowp added as qualifiers. 3 compile errors fixed but 3 spirv-val failures added. The new failures are from files that now compile but produce invalid SPIR-V due to other issues. Pattern: each infrastructure improvement moves files from compile_error to spirv-val, not to pass.
asi.next_action_hint: The key bottleneck is that many files that compile produce invalid SPIR-V. Need to fix the SPIR-V quality — type mismatches, undefined IDs, wrong result types for builtins. Focus on the 32 spirv-val failures and try to convert some to passes.

description: Fix compound_assign *= for matrix types. 60→61 passes (+1).

asi.findings: compound_assign mul_assign now properly dispatches mat*mat, mat*vec, vec*mat operations. The generic fmul was producing 'Expected floating scalar or vector type' for matrix result types. rmw-matrix.comp now validates.
asi.next_action_hint: Check if the binary_op handler has the same issue — mat*mat should use mat_mat_mul, not fmul. Also check vec*scalar and scalar*vec in binary_op.

description: Session 7 consolidation. 61 passes (+1).

asi.findings: Session 7: multiple infrastructure improvements but only +1 net pass. Main blocker: [] parsing in uniform/buffer blocks causes regressions every time. The 105 compile errors and 31 spirv-val failures are dominated by missing array support and type mismatches. The barrier builtins, precision qualifiers, sampler2DShadow, image2D are all infrastructure that doesn't immediately help pass count but sets up for future improvements.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on converting spirv-val failures to passes. The 31 failures include: 6 'Operand requires previous definition' (phantom IDs), 3 'block must end with branch' (parser issues), 2 'execution model' (capability), 2 'duplicate type' (ID collision), type mismatches. Each fixed spirv-val failure could be a pass.

description: Dedup OpTypeFunction declarations. 61→62 passes (+1).

asi.findings: OpTypeFunction dedup via hash cache. Each void() function was getting its own OpTypeFunction with different IDs but same content. SPIR-V disallows duplicate non-aggregate type declarations. Simple FNV-like hash of return_type_id + param_type_ids for cache key.
asi.next_action_hint: outer-product.comp also has 'Duplicate TypeMatrix' — check if OpTypeMatrix has the same dedup issue in ensureType. Also check the 4 'Invalid OpExtInst word count' files — modf/frexp have wrong operand counts.

description: Don't OpLoad function parameters. 62→63 passes (+2).

asi.findings: Function params don't need OpLoad — OpFunctionParameter already gives the value. Loading from a sampler2D param caused 'not a pointer type' error. Removing load for params fixed 2 spirv-val failures. Only var_sym (global/local variables) need loads since they're OpVariable pointers.
asi.next_action_hint: Check if the 4 'Invalid OpExtInst word count' files can be fixed by handling modf/frexp specially. Also check 'outer-product.comp' duplicate TypeMatrix.

description: Fix GLSL.std.450 instruction numbers + outerProduct/transpose + matrix dedup. 63→64

asi.findings: 15 GLSL.std.450 instruction numbers were wrong. outerProduct was being sent as ext_inst #53 (PackSnorm4x8!) which spirv-val interpreted as Ldexp. Matrix type dedup had mat2/mat2x2 alias collision. numColumns/columnType were swapped for non-square matrices. OpVectorTimesScalar opcode is 142, not 114 (ConvertSToU).
asi.next_action_hint: Check remaining 28 spirv-val failures. Many may now be fixed since the instruction number fixes affect all shaders using max/clamp/mix/step/smoothstep/distance/length/cross/normalize/reflect/refract/inverse/fma/frexp/ldexp.

description: Fix remaining GLSL.std.450 instruction numbers + add dot/derivative core ops. 64→65

asi.findings: The old glslExtInstruction function had BOTH old and new code — my earlier fix only replaced half. Now completely replaced. dot uses OpDot (core opcode 148), dFdx/dFdy use DPdx/DPdy (207/208). modf still has wrong word count (passes 2 args to ModfStruct which takes 1).
asi.next_action_hint: Fix modf/frexp word count (5 files). Fix modf to use ModfStruct with 1 arg + separate store.

description: Add int-to-float conversion in compound_assign for vec*int. 65→66

asi.findings: spv.430.frag was failing because color *= gl_Layer generated FMul(vec4, int). Added int→float conversion + splat to vector in compound_assign handler. Cache invalidation is tricky — need rm -rf .zig-cache when exe disappears.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix layout-component.desktop.frag (same FMul type issue). Fix transpose.legacy.vert FMul. Fix phantom IDs. Fix 'block must end with branch'.

description: Fix promoteTypes for matrix*scalar + add mat_scalar_mul/scalar_mat_mul. 66→67

asi.findings: promoteTypes(mat4, float) returned .float due to early 'a==.float or b==.float' check. Must check matrix/scalar promotion BEFORE the generic float fallback. OpMatrixTimesScalar (opcode 143) handles mat*scalar.
asi.next_action_hint: Check remaining 25 spirv-val failures. layout-component.desktop.frag has same FAdd issue. transpose.legacy.vert might now pass.

description: Scalar-to-vector splat in binary_op for add/sub/div. Infrastructure.

asi.findings: Added scalar-to-vector splat in binary_op for add/sub/div ops. layout-component.desktop.frag now passes but was already counted. No regression. The splat uses OpCompositeConstruct with N copies of the scalar ID.
asi.next_action_hint: Look at outer-product.comp VectorTimesScalar type mismatch. Fix pls.frag VectorTimesScalar type issue. Fix mix.frag FMix type issue. These are concrete spirv-val failures.

description: Fix outerProduct result type direction. 67→68 passes (+1)

asi.findings: outerProduct result type was backwards. outerProduct(vecN, vecM) produces a matrix with N rows and M columns = matMxN in GLSL (M cols of vecN). Had to swap a_rows/b_rows in type selection. Also affects pls.frag which had same VectorTimesScalar type mismatch.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix remaining 24 spirv-val failures. Key categories: phantom IDs (4), block-must-end-with-branch (4), execution model mismatch (2), FMix operand types, ext_inst word count, OpTypePointer in wrong section, duplicate IDs.
